claim: Prompt engineering entire modules by specifying function prototypes and descriptions to GPT is faster than manual coding - you can get 80% of a working implementation from a single prompt
actionable: When building Python classes or modules, write a detailed prompt with function signatures, parameter names, and descriptions of expected behavior; feed to GPT-4 to generate functional code
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent

claim: Combining transcribed table definitions from the database with prompt instructions helps LLMs generate accurate SQL without needing explicit training on your schema
actionable: For database query agents, fetch CREATE TABLE statements from the database and include them in prompts to LLMs so they understand your exact schema structure
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent

claim: Using delimiter-based response formatting (like dashes) in prompts helps reliably parse SQL queries from LLM outputs without JSON schema validation
actionable: Instruct LLMs to wrap SQL queries between delimiter markers (e.g., `---SQL---` and `---END---`) to enable reliable string parsing of structured output
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent

claim: Aider (AI pair programmer) can be used to generate the main application logic after you've set up modules via prompt engineering, completing ~80% of a full application
actionable: Use Aider after you've generated base modules with prompt engineering to write application orchestration code; provide comments describing desired behavior
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent

claim: Natural language to SQL translation works best when you split the pipeline: first collect schema, then construct a prompt with table definitions, then query the LLM, then parse the response
actionable: Build a modular pipeline: (1) fetch schema from DB, (2) inject into prompt template, (3) send to LLM, (4) parse SQL from response, (5) execute on DB
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent

claim: Getting LLMs to return responses in a specific consistent format requires iterative prompt refinement - the exact phrasing of format instructions significantly impacts success rate
actionable: When prompt engineering SQL generation, test multiple phrasings of the format instruction; if output doesn't parse cleanly, add extra newlines or adjust delimiter descriptions
source_title: Prompt Engineering an ENTIRE codebase: Postgres Data Analytics AI Agent
